[2018-02-14 17:52] supergreyman (Greyman): Elyot is thinking of implementing a puzzle maker in the future, which you can use it to create custom units (as shown in the stream). So in a way, you can create a scenario where you can play with it against a Master bot.
[2018-02-14 17:58] apooche: If the interface is simplified I don't know 🤷‍♀️ if there would be custom unit creation. I think at some point ☝️ Elyot said he didn't want any custom unit creation modes in the game.
[2018-02-14 18:03] supergreyman (Greyman): Why not? Would it become a problem?
[2018-02-14 18:06] supergreyman (Greyman): Other games like Starcraft lets you make custom maps and units to be shared with the community. Would it be different with Prismata?
[2018-02-14 18:07] crash_overlord: Comparing starcraft development resources and prismatas
[2018-02-14 18:09] supergreyman (Greyman): Well, in case Loonark became rich after Steam release.
[2018-02-14 18:17] kaiasky (kaia): I mean it is risky, you don't want to split your already potentially small community among too many different modes
[2018-02-14 18:20] .jrkirby (jrkirby): @Crash_overlord Warcraft 2 had a custom map editor, and you actually could compare 1995 Blizzard's dev resources to Lunarch's
[2018-02-14 18:23] darksado (Raylin): Hi guys, I have been disconnected for the last 3 weeks. At that time, Elyot said the early access would probably happen this week. Do you have a date in mind for the early access?
[2018-02-14 18:23] elyot: we're still waiting on Valve to approve our Steam page
[2018-02-14 18:23] elyot: we had to make a number of fixes
[2018-02-14 18:23] elyot: once it's approved then we will announce a date
[2018-02-14 18:23] elyot: I believe that Valve will make us wait 14 days
[2018-02-14 18:25] darksado (Raylin): thanks for the answer. Looking forward to that. And I am glad that everything is ready. It is important to have a first good impression hehe
[2018-02-14 18:25] elyot: Regarding custom units and custom puzzles... if we ever implement something, it will be suuuuper janky because we don't *have* a real puzzle editor. It's just me and I just edit in json. WarCraft's map editor was a tool built by a tools programmer for use by their design team
[2018-02-14 18:26] elyot: We don't even have a proper spec for it
[2018-02-14 18:26] supergreyman (Greyman): I remember you once said that releasing Prismata on March would be pretty disadvantageous. How bad would it be?
[2018-02-14 18:29] elyot: March is usually a bad month but everything is late this year, PAX East not even until april and GDC is march 18 or so, thronebreaker seems hella delayed, only thing I'm worried about is artifact atm
[2018-02-14 18:29] elyot: sea of thieves march 20 and a bunch of other things that week
[2018-02-14 18:30] elyot: but there's not much we can do, literally it's just in "waiting for review" mode
[2018-02-14 18:31] elyot: I'm grateful to have a little extra time regardless, our PR campaign is gonna be siiick
[2018-02-14 18:32] elyot: but honestly, if you look at any video game release calendar, it's all remakes and bullshit until GDC and PAX East
[2018-02-14 18:33] elyot: Stellaris ended up decided to release their expansion on the original date we had picked too lol
[2018-02-14 18:36] supergreyman (Greyman): Will the PR campaign slogan be:
*Could you lead a massive army to victory*❓
[2018-02-14 18:36] elyot: They sent me 2 press releases this morning to review
[2018-02-14 18:37] elyot: They looked quite good though I will be making some small edits
[2018-02-14 18:37] supergreyman (Greyman): Looking forward to it! 😃
[2018-02-14 18:37] crash_overlord: *Could you lead a massive army to the victory* ❓ ❗

[2018-03-02 12:55] saracen26: @jrkirby If this game runs on AWS which I think it does. It's down to Security group rules. Ports reference SQL databases as well as whether the inbound/outbound traffic is IPv4 or IPv6 and whether the connection access is HTTP or HTTPS. A port can be given to the protocol connection type and ports can also be assigned for Elastic Load balancing.

As far as I'm aware because of all the different factors that go into connecting with such a server, you're not able to push all that through a single port.
[2018-03-02 13:04] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It definitely does run through AWS. That's the IP on the other side of the socket. But the sockets I was looking at weren't HTTP or HTTPS, they were just raw, unencrypted TCP traffic. But you might be right about the elastic load balancing causing the port numbers.
[2018-03-02 13:09] saracen26: Yeah from what I'm aware it's usually recommended to set-up backend ports alongside the typical Public facing port... I'm not a server guru and only have a basic understanding. But I believe with load balancing the public port balances across all of these ports.
[2018-03-02 13:09] saracen26: Yeah just found an example on the AWS forum

```Bind the public facing ELB port to 80
Bind the backend ports to 8001, 8002, 8003, 8004, 8005
Run two large instance types behind the ELB
Each large instance runs 5 of the same docker web apps 
Each docker web app binds to one of the ports (8001, 8002, 8003, 8004, 8005)
The ELB would balance the requests across each of the ports.```
[2018-03-02 13:12] saracen26: It's effectively a Docker.

messages.author: saracen26
messages.content:
@jrkirby If this game runs on AWS which I think it does. It's down to Security group rules. Ports reference SQL databases as well as whether the inbound/outbound traffic is IPv4 or IPv6 and whether the connection access is HTTP or HTTPS. A port can be given to the protocol connection type and ports can also be assigned for Elastic Load balancing.

As far as I'm aware because of all the different factors that go into connecting with such a server, you're not able to push all that through a single port.

messages.author: .jrkirby
messages.content: It definitely does run through AWS. That's the IP on the other side of the socket. But the sockets I was looking at weren't HTTP or HTTPS, they were just raw, unencrypted TCP traffic. But you might be right about the elastic load balancing causing the port numbers.

messages.author: saracen26
messages.content: Yeah from what I'm aware it's usually recommended to set-up backend ports alongside the typical Public facing port... I'm not a server guru and only have a basic understanding. But I believe with load balancing the public port balances across all of these ports.

messages.author: saracen26
messages.content:
Yeah just found an example on the AWS forum

```Bind the public facing ELB port to 80
Bind the backend ports to 8001, 8002, 8003, 8004, 8005
Run two large instance types behind the ELB
Each large instance runs 5 of the same docker web apps 
Each docker web app binds to one of the ports (8001, 8002, 8003, 8004, 8005)
The ELB would balance the requests across each of the ports.```

messages.content: It's effectively a Docker.
